I think she's entitled to feel a little hurt.

Ralf Schumacher's ex-wife has aimed a thinly veiled dig at him over his new marriage to a man, with new photos having emerged of the ceremony. The former F1 driver, who is brother to stricken legendary former Ferrari star Michael, got married to Etienne Bousquet-Cassagne in Saint-Tropez at the weekend. Ralf, 50, and Etienne, 36, were joined in matrimony just 19 months after the ex-driver came out as gay and his ex-wife Cora made a thinly veiled dig while purporting to offer her best wishes.
  • 261 comments

Meanwhile, as evidenced by PMQs today, the self anoited political elite want to bury their hands in the sand

Police are poised for further clashes after eleven officers and a dog were injured during riots in Southampton sparked by the treatment of Henry Nowak. Public anger was sparked by bodycam footage showing officers handcuffing the 18-year-old university student as he lay dying after his Sikh killer falsely claimed to be a victim of racism. Hampshire Constabulary have increased patrols in Southampton and warned of further arrests following the 'unacceptable scenes', which saw thugs pelting officers with glass bottles. Mr Nowak's case has also prompted a political row as 'two-tier' guidance treating white people differently to ethnic minorities was blamed for the way officers responded. The teenager was stabbed six times by Sikh stranger Vickrum Digwa, 23, who lied to officers that he had shouted racist abuse, punched him and knocked off his turban. Mr Nowak repeatedly told officers 'I can't breathe' before his death.
  • 833 comments
  • 4 videos
  • share Police brace for more Henry Nowak protests after 11 officers injured

EXCLUSIVEThis is a dangerous and irresponsible article. It is true that there is evidence that being pregnant when under 35 slightly reduces the chances of getting breast cancer - slightly. It fails to say that pregnancy increases the risk - slightly - for a short period after the pregnancy. It is utterly irresponsible for anyone to say "‘Biologically, women are ready for pregnancy soon after their first period..." Some girls are starting their periods at 8 or 9, most below the age of 15, when their bodies are still growing. Biologically they can get pregnant but physically their bodies aren't ready and their minds certainly are not. This is the attitude some men use to justify very young marriages. It is wrong.

'People are reluctant to talk about,' says Dr Andrea DeCensi, director of medical oncology at Galliera Hospital. 'It's one of the biggest contributors to rising breast cancer rates.'
  • 25 comments

Welcome to mansworld Brad

Brad Pitt looked in complete shock as he watched the TT racing on the Isle of Man while his new movie starring Channing Tatum kicked off filming on Wednesday.  The Hollywood actor landed on the island by private jet and quickly joined motorcycle enthusiasts to watch the first Supersport race of the year, which saw Michael Dunlop extend his record number of wins at the road race to 34. Brad was filmed lying on a wall joining the 50,000 spectators overlooking the track, which takes place on closed public roads over the 37.7 mile (60km) Mountain Course.

NEW This is the moment a Sikh man shook hands and fist bumped protesters who were marching amid mounting anger over the murder of student Henry Nowak. Kulwinder Singh, a 47-year-old taxi driver from Southampton, welcomed the protest in the city and those who were marching following the 18-year-old's death. He said: 'I'm part of the Sikh faith so I welcome them. They are my brothers and sisters. I'm not worried at all, they're welcome at the Gurdwara [Sikh temple].'

My father in-law had Alzheimer's and my mother in-law looked after him at home without any outside help apart from my wife and close family. When the day came for him to go into a care home. It was one of the saddest days watching my mother in-law grieving him going. But it was the best thing that ever happened in regards to him being cared for by care home staff. I really wonder how my mother in-law managed for so long. We could see her going down hill but her love for him put that aside. So please don't be too quick to have a swipe at someone going through the same situation. It's heartbreaking to see .

War has broken out amongst leaders of the Traveller community with the Appleby Horse Fair organiser dubbed an 'informant' for backing the introduction of live facial recognition cameras at the event to crackdown on troublemakers. Hundreds of Travellers have been seen arriving in Appleby today in their horse-drawn wagons ahead of the historical horse trading event which begins tomorrow and will run until June 10. Cumbria Police's Fire and Crime Commissioner David Allen announced the controversial AI-powered technology would be deployed for the first time at Europe's largest traditional Gypsy fair after a record number of arrests were made last year.

  • My father in-law had Alzheimer's and my mother in-law looked after him at home without any outside help apart from my wife and close family. When the day came for him to go into a care home. It was one of the saddest days watching my mother in-law grieving him going. But it was the best thing that ever happened in regards to him being cared for by care home staff. I really wonder how my mother in-law managed for so long. We could see her going down hill but her love for him put that aside. So please don't be too quick to have a swipe at someone going through the same situation. It's heartbreaking to see .

  • I really love these two together! So fundamentally different but they both seem hardworking, kind and share good values. I also love how he doesn’t seem jealous of her success, and praises her so thoughtfully. 🥰

  • I feel sorry for her ex-husband and kids. It’s great that she’s working on herself but alcoholism is an extremely selfish disease and the family often suffers. When they’re drinking it’s all about them and when they are trying to be sober it’s still all about them. You’re whole existence and daily experiences are based their sobriety (or not). There’s a fine line between being supportive and enabling, and getting blamed for not being supportive enough. Then when they do finally get sober for extended amount of time they get empowered and decide they don’t need you anymore. You fought and fought all for nought. Wish her the best, hope she can rebuild a solid relationship with her kids but the past painful experiences they will always remember.
  • Can you use equity release to fund buying a second home? Ad Feature
  • As beautiful as Alan's Georgian home & gardens are, I would have had a For Sale sign up ASAP had I lived there. Part of the beauty would have been the open view of the fields, and also the sense of space. Imagine how noisy the area will become once the new properties are built & sold. They likelihood is that every new house will have a car, possibly two or even three, revving up every morning, lawn mowers making a racket at weekends, children shreking as they play outside - it's going to be an invasion of noise. It won't bother the folks who are actually living on the new development, but it isn't what the existing community signed up for when they bought their homes, many decades ago. Noise pollution. There ought to be a law against it !
  • What rubbish. These buffoons, who seem unable to be without their mobile phones even for the duration of a performance should not go to the theatre, as they ruin it for the other spectators sitting around them. EXCLUSIVEMade In Chelsea's Ollie Locke and his family took refuge at a friend's home after they took pity following 'humiliating' eviction from £4million home over unpaid rent bill - before fleeing London for a new life in the country

Made in Chelsea star Ollie Locke and his husband Gareth took refuge at the home of a longtime friend who took pity on them after being evicted from their £4million home for failing to pay rent, we can reveal. The reality TV couple were forced out of their Chelsea townhouse earlier this year amid a mounting rent scandal that has left a £45,000 bill still unpaid. The couple and their two-year-old twins, Apollo and Cosima, were last month spotted at another rental property in affluent south-west London enclave Barnes, a stone's throw from Ollie's best friend Binky Felstead.

Relight my fire! Robbie Williams and Gary Barlow take to the stage together for first time in a decade as they perform Take That hit with Lulu in show honouring her at Royal Albert Hall

Lulu had a reunion with Take That on Monday to perform their hit track Relight My Fire at London's Royal Albert Hall. Lulu, 77, returned to the stage for a one-off show to celebrate her career alongside a string of celeb pals, with Gary Barlow, 55, and Robbie Williams, 52, joining her on stage.  The performance also marked the first time former foes Gary and Robbie had performed together in nearly ten years.

Missed opportunity to use the Benny Hill soundtrack.

A Japanese man was chased down and mauled by a bear before it smashed through a glass door in a violent attack.  Police and fire department officials rushed to the scene in the Sasakino district of Fukushima in northeastern Japan on Tuesday after receiving an emergency call from the Fukushima Steel Works reporting bear attacks on two employees. Security camera footage shows a black bear appearing and chasing an employee near the entrance.
  • 132 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Bear mauls man before smashing through glass door in Japan

Email

I have a dozen or so parakeets visiting my garden daily along with many other varieties of birds. They all eat the same food which I provide for them, and the parakeets don't cause any damage at all. The poor thing is just hungry!

They are normally found in the tropical regions of Africa and admired for their colourful plumage.
  • 61 comments
  • share Escaped parrot goes on year-long wrecking spree

Great photo, but dandelion?? Funniest dandelion I have ever seen

The picture was taken in the grasslands of Lower Austria by wildlife photographer Perdita Peltz. Ground squirrels spend hours foraging for food to build up their fat reserves in order to survive the winter. During hibernation, the squirrels use up around 90 per cent of their body fat reserves accumulated during the summer.
  • 51 comments
  • share Sweet snap shows squirrels sharing a dandelion
